---
title: "Donchian Channel Breakout Strategy"
subtitle: "Systematic trend-following on GLD (SPDR Gold Trust)"
author: "FinTech 533"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    page-layout: article
---

::: {.hero}
# Donchian Channel Breakout on GLD
A transparent, rules-based trend-following system that enters on 20-bar price breakouts, sizes exits with ATR, and caps risk with a hard time-out.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| code-fold: true
#| label: run-backtest
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

ROOT = Path(".").resolve()
DATA = ROOT / "data"

# Regenerate artifacts fresh on every render
subprocess.run([sys.executable, "scripts/backtest.py"], check=True)

blotter = pd.read_csv(DATA / "trade_blotter.csv", parse_dates=["entry_date", "exit_date"])
equity  = pd.read_csv(DATA / "equity.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
prices  = pd.read_csv(DATA / "price_data.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
with open(DATA / "metrics.json") as f:
    metrics = json.load(f)
```

## Strategy Logic

The strategy watches a rolling 20-day high/low envelope — the classic Donchian channel — and waits for price to decisively pierce one of the boundaries. A close above the upper band signals that recent resistance has broken, the path of least resistance is up, and we go long the next morning; a close below the lower band does the symmetric thing on the short side. Every trade carries a pre-computed profit target of two ATRs and a stop-loss of one ATR from the entry, giving a 2:1 reward-to-risk geometry; if neither boundary is touched within twenty trading days the position is closed at the market so capital never sits idle in a range-bound trade. Only one position is open at a time, slippage and commission are charged on both legs, and no pyramiding is permitted.

## Asset Selection

The candidate universe was an 8-ETF basket spanning equities, bonds, and commodities: SPY, QQQ, IWM, TLT, GLD, USO, XLE, and EEM. For each ETF we counted how many 20-bar Donchian breakouts occurred over a 3-year sample and divided by bar count to get a breakout frequency. **GLD** screened best: commodities in general showed stronger trending behaviour than equities over this window, and gold specifically produced the cleanest alternation of decisive trends and clear consolidations — exactly the regime that rewards channel breakouts. GLD is also highly liquid (tight spreads, large ADV) so the slippage assumption below is conservative.

## Breakout Definition

A **breakout** is defined mechanically so there is zero discretion:

- **Upper band** = highest high of the prior **20 bars** (`LOOKBACK_PERIOD = 20`).
- **Lower band** = lowest low of the prior **20 bars**.
- **Long signal** fires when today's close > upper band; the trade is filled at the next bar's open.
- **Short signal** fires when today's close < lower band; filled at the next bar's open.
- **ATR** uses Wilder smoothing over **14 bars** (`ATR_PERIOD = 14`).
- **Profit target** = entry ± **2.0 × ATR** (`ATR_MULT = 2.0`).
- **Stop-loss** = entry ∓ **1.0 × ATR** (`STOP_MULT = 1.0`).
- **Time-out** = force-close at the close of bar 20 after entry (`MAX_HOLDING_DAYS = 20`).
- **Size** = **100 shares** per trade (`POSITION_SIZE = 100`).
- **Costs** = **0.1% slippage + 0.1% commission** applied on both entry and exit sides.
- **Risk-free rate** = **5.3%** annualised, used for Sharpe / Sortino (`RISK_FREE_RATE = 0.053`).

All channel values are shifted by one bar so only prior-bar information is ever used — no peek-ahead.

## Equity Curve

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-equity
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=equity["date"], y=equity["equity"],
    mode="lines", name="Cumulative PnL",
    line=dict(color="#1f4e79", width=2),
    fill="tozeroy", fillcolor="rgba(31,78,121,0.12)",
))
# shaded drawdown band
running_max = equity["equity"].cummax()
drawdown = equity["equity"] - running_max
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=equity["date"], y=drawdown,
    mode="lines", name="Drawdown",
    line=dict(color="#c0392b", width=1, dash="dot"),
    yaxis="y2",
))
fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=420,
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=30, b=40),
    yaxis=dict(title="Cumulative PnL ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Drawdown ($)", overlaying="y", side="right", showgrid=False),
    xaxis=dict(title=""),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.1, x=0),
)
fig.show()
```

## Donchian Channel Chart

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-donchian
# keep the chart readable — last ~500 bars plus indicator overlays and trade markers
px_df = prices.tail(500).copy()

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Candlestick(
    x=px_df["date"],
    open=px_df["open"], high=px_df["high"],
    low=px_df["low"],   close=px_df["close"],
    name="GLD",
    increasing_line_color="#1f8f5a",
    decreasing_line_color="#c0392b",
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=px_df["date"], y=px_df["donchian_upper"],
    mode="lines", name="Donchian Upper",
    line=dict(color="#2d6cb3", width=1.2),
))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=px_df["date"], y=px_df["donchian_lower"],
    mode="lines", name="Donchian Lower",
    line=dict(color="#b33a3a", width=1.2),
))

# trade markers within the visible window
visible_trades = blotter[
    (blotter["entry_date"] >= px_df["date"].min()) &
    (blotter["entry_date"] <= px_df["date"].max())
]
if not visible_trades.empty:
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=visible_trades["entry_date"], y=visible_trades["entry_price"],
        mode="markers", name="Entry",
        marker=dict(symbol="triangle-up", color="#1f4e79", size=10, line=dict(color="white", width=1)),
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=visible_trades["exit_date"], y=visible_trades["exit_price"],
        mode="markers", name="Exit",
        marker=dict(symbol="x", color="#444", size=9),
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=520,
    margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=30, b=40),
    xaxis=dict(title="", rangeslider=dict(visible=False)),
    yaxis=dict(title="Price ($)"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.08, x=0),
)
fig.show()
```

## Performance Metrics

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: tbl-metrics
rows = [
    ("Number of trades",         f"{metrics['n_trades']}",                    "Total completed round-trips in the test window."),
    ("Win rate",                 f"{metrics['win_rate']*100:.2f}%",           "Share of trades with positive net return after costs."),
    ("Average return per trade", f"{metrics['avg_return_pct']:.2f}%",         "Mean of all trade returns, winners and losers combined."),
    ("Average winning trade",    f"{metrics['avg_win_pct']:.2f}%",            "Mean return across trades that closed in profit."),
    ("Average losing trade",     f"{metrics['avg_loss_pct']:.2f}%",           "Mean return across trades that closed at a loss or flat."),
    ("Profit factor",            f"{metrics['profit_factor']:.2f}",           "Gross profit divided by gross loss — above 1 is profitable."),
    ("Sharpe ratio (annualised)",  f"{metrics['sharpe']:.2f}",                "Risk-adjusted return using total equity-curve volatility."),
    ("Sortino ratio (annualised)", f"{metrics['sortino']:.2f}",               "Like Sharpe but penalises only downside volatility."),
    ("Max drawdown",             f"${metrics['max_drawdown']:.2f}",           "Largest peak-to-trough equity decline in the test window."),
    ("Total PnL",                f"${metrics['total_pnl']:.2f}",              "Net dollar profit/loss after slippage and commission."),
]
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["Metric", "Value", "What it means"])
from IPython.display import HTML
HTML(metrics_df.to_html(index=False, classes="metrics-table", escape=False))
```

## Trade Outcome Analysis

Every position is forced to close through one of three doors: the **profit target** (entry ± 2 × ATR), the **stop-loss** (entry ∓ 1 × ATR), or a **time-out** after 20 trading days. The stop-loss is tested each bar against the bar's low (for longs) or high (for shorts); if the stop and the target are both reachable on the same bar, the stop is assumed to fill first — a conservative choice that avoids flattering the numbers.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-outcomes
if blotter.empty:
    print("No trades generated in the test window.")
else:
    counts = blotter["outcome"].value_counts().reindex(
        ["Profit target", "Stop-loss", "Time-out"], fill_value=0
    ).reset_index()
    counts.columns = ["Outcome", "Count"]
    fig = px.bar(
        counts, x="Outcome", y="Count",
        color="Outcome",
        color_discrete_map={
            "Profit target": "#1f8f5a",
            "Stop-loss":     "#c0392b",
            "Time-out":      "#5d6d7e",
        },
        text="Count",
    )
    fig.update_traces(textposition="outside")
    fig.update_layout(
        template="plotly_white", height=380,
        margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=30, b=40),
        showlegend=False,
        yaxis=dict(title="Number of trades"),
        xaxis=dict(title=""),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## Trade Blotter

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: tbl-blotter
from IPython.display import HTML

display_cols = [
    "entry_date", "exit_date", "entry_price", "exit_price",
    "quantity", "direction", "outcome", "pnl", "trade_return",
]
if blotter.empty:
    HTML("<p><em>No trades executed in the test window.</em></p>")
else:
    view = blotter[display_cols].copy()
    view["entry_date"] = view["entry_date"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    view["exit_date"]  = view["exit_date"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    view["trade_return"] = (view["trade_return"] * 100).round(3).astype(str) + "%"

    table_html = view.to_html(
        index=False, table_id="trade-blotter", classes="display compact",
        escape=False,
    )
    datatable_script = """
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.datatables.net/1.13.6/css/jquery.dataTables.min.css" />
<script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.7.1.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.datatables.net/1.13.6/js/jquery.dataTables.min.js"></script>
<script>
$(document).ready(function () {
  $('#trade-blotter').DataTable({
    pageLength: 15,
    order: [[0, 'asc']],
    lengthMenu: [[10, 15, 25, 50, -1], [10, 15, 25, 50, "All"]]
  });
});
</script>
"""
    HTML(table_html + datatable_script)
```

## Download

::: {.download-link}
[Download trade_blotter.csv](data/trade_blotter.csv)
:::

## Assumptions

- **Slippage**: 0.1% of fill price applied on every entry and exit.
- **Commission**: 0.1% per side, in addition to slippage.
- **No pyramiding**: only one position is held at any time; new signals during an open trade are ignored.
- **Data source**: live GLD daily bars from Interactive Brokers TWS (`ib_insync`); if TWS is unreachable the pipeline falls back to a synthetic GBM series with GLD-like parameters so the site always renders.
- **No dividends**: GLD distributions are ignored; returns are price-only.
- **Walk-forward**: year 1 is used for indicator warm-up, years 2–3 are the out-of-sample test window reported above.
- **Single-instrument**: results do not account for portfolio-level diversification or correlation effects.
